task_name: Scrub Neon resource identifiers from BP-131 files
goal: Remove the Neon resource identifiers left in the two BP-131 files so the working tree is identifier-free; replace each with a neutral placeholder while keeping the documents readable. No secret or connection string was ever present. The Neon default database name and role are left as-is because they are product defaults, not resource coordinates.
scope_out: Edit only the two BP-131 files plus the anchors; do not touch any other contract or review packet.
Do not change Builder code, tests, packages, or soulmatch.
Do not attempt to rewrite Git history; that is a separate decision and out of scope.
Do not remove the human label bluepilot-builder, which is not sensitive.
Do not change the Neon default values neondb and neondb_owner; they are product defaults, not coordinates to this project.
claims: In contracts/BP-131.json and review-packets/BP-131.md, the Neon project ID, branch ID and compute/endpoint identifier are replaced with neutral placeholders (<neon-project-id>, <neon-branch-id>, <neon-endpoint>), and the soulmatch project ID is replaced with <soulmatch-project-id>.

The database name (neondb) and role (neondb_owner) are Neon product defaults, not resource coordinates that point to this project, and are left unchanged. The human label bluepilot-builder is also kept.
The documents stay readable: the table structure and meaning are preserved, only the concrete identifiers are removed.
No secret or connection string is added; none existed.
After the scrub, a tree-wide scan for the known identifiers returns nothing.
assumptions: The known identifier strings are the concrete project, branch, endpoint, and old soulmatch project values currently present in the two BP-131 files. They are intentionally not repeated in this contract.
Git history still contains these strings; closing history fully would need a rewrite or a private repo and is intentionally not done here.
dod: git grep over the whole tree for the four known identifier strings returns no matches in the working tree.
contracts/BP-131.json remains valid JSON after the scrub.
review-packets/BP-131.md remains readable with placeholders in place of the identifiers.
Only the two BP-131 files plus contracts/BP-134.json, review-packets/BP-134.md, STATE.md, and docs/SESSION-LOG.md are changed.
review-packets/BP-134.md notes that the working tree is now identifier-free and that Git history is unchanged by design.
No Builder code/test/package or soulmatch file is changed.
stop_conditions: The scrub would require touching a file other than the two BP-131 files and the anchors.
Removing an identifier would break BP-131.json as valid JSON in a way that cannot be fixed with a placeholder.
A Builder code/test/package or soulmatch change becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BP-133 anchored the live deploy and scrubbed identifiers from STATE.md and docs/SESSION-LOG.md, but VAL-K2 found the Neon identifiers still present in contracts/BP-131.json (project_id, branch_id) and review-packets/BP-131.md (project ID, branch ID, compute endpoint, soulmatch project ID). No password or connection string was ever committed. BP-134 finishes the working-tree scrub without repeating the concrete identifiers in this contract. Note: Git history still contains the identifiers; a full history clean would need a rewrite or making the repo private, which is a separate decision.
